methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
1X Technologies is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Bernt Øivind Børnich that has raised $123.5M to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $10.0B. The comparable universe consists of 9 private robotics companies at similar stages and 1 public comp (277810.KQ).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to Series C advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $19.0B from the $10.0B last post-money. The peer-median method examines five same-sector, same-stage peers—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M)—producing a median valuation of $500M and a range of $300M to $1.8B.

The two methods produce a wide triangulated range of $425M (low) to $21.9B (high), with a median of $9.8B. The current $10.0B valuation sits near this median but substantially above the peer-median baseline. The material divergence between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects either a valuation premium relative to current-stage robotics comps or suggests that peer benchmarks may not adequately capture 1X's specific market position. Key uncertainties include whether the target's technology differentiation and capital efficiency justify its valuation premium relative to the peer set.
